source: independent (non-author) pred-scout-coach-critic reviewing data/postgame/*.json coaching against the match facts; preference-based / ungrounded lines flagged; grounded rewrites applied back

26301393-a499-4c6f-9210-b5cde5fffe85.quote: Both mid and carry were read as theirs all game, and Murdock (49k physical) was a monster — they out-killed you on objectives 27 to 22. You lost a long string of fights to him: the 12.2 Fangtooth (1-4), the 20.3 Fangtooth (0-2), a 23.7 open-map bad trade (0-3), and the 27.4 Orb Prime fight (1-4). What saved it was refusing to keep flipping the same fight and instead grouping late — you out-damaged them on objectives overall (92k to 42k) and won the 35.5 Orb Prime fight (3-2) and the 38.1 close (3-0). The lesson is the 23.7 bad trade: a 3-man into Murdock and Crunch in the open with nothing up was a free loss. Two of you built armor against 49k physical — that should have been three.
26301393-a499-4c6f-9210-b5cde5fffe85.issue: macro says 23.7 was opened 5v4 in your favor and traded for Primal Fangtooth — not a 3-man free loss into Murdock and Crunch
26301393-a499-4c6f-9210-b5cde5fffe85.rewrite: Both mid and carry were read as theirs all game, and Murdock (49k physical) was a monster — they out-killed you on objectives 27 to 22. You lost a long string of fights to him: the 12.2 Fangtooth (1-4), the 20.3 Fangtooth (0-2), a 23.7 open-map bad trade (0-3), and the 27.4 Orb Prime fight (1-4). What saved it was refusing to keep flipping the same fight and instead grouping late — you out-damaged them on objectives overall (92k to 42k) and won the 35.5 Orb Prime fight (3-2) and the 38.1 close (3-0). The lesson is the 23.7 bad trade: you had the bodies (5v4) and still went 0-3 in the open — you took Primal Fangtooth in the same window, so read it as a trade, but a fight you open with the man up is one you should win outright. Two of you built armor against 49k physical — that should have been three.

3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.quote: On paper this should have been hard — only mid was favored, and your offlane, jungle and support were all read as theirs all game. You won it on objective tempo and discipline: back-to-back won fights at your Fangtooth (8.5 and 9 min, 2-0 each) flipped the early map, and from there you took the lead in objective kills (19 to 15). The two clean losses were the lesson — a 4v5 you flipped in the open at 20.4 (0-3, nobody to kill) and a needless 2v1 at 28 where Gideon and Phase fed Eden for nothing. Both were dumb battles with no major up; reset and farm instead. The close-out was textbook: you grouped on Primal Fangtooth at 31.7 and aced four of them, then ended.
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.issue: macro says 20.4 and 28 were both 5v5 — the '4v5', the '2v1' and the Gideon/Phase-died-to-Eden attribution are invented
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.rewrite: On paper this should have been hard — only mid was favored, and your offlane, jungle and support were all read as theirs all game. You won it on objective tempo and discipline: back-to-back won fights at your Fangtooth (8.5 and 9 min, 2-0 each) flipped the early map, and from there you took the lead in objective kills (19 to 15). The two clean losses were the lesson — the 20.4 open-map 0-3 came while Gideon was ahead in lane and absent, and the 28 open-map 0-2 came while Sparrow was ahead and absent. Both were fights with no major up; reset and farm instead. The close-out was textbook: you grouped on Primal Fangtooth at 31.7 and aced four of them, then ended.

3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.quote: Your Gideon won mid outright and it carried the early game — kills on Renna at 5.1 and 8.5 kept the favored lane snowballing while the rest of the map was even. The two deaths to watch both came in lost fights with nothing on the line: the 28-min 2v1 where you and Phase died to a single Eden was a free trade given away. When no major is up, don't take that.
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.issue: invented kill timeline (no 5.1 event in SOURCE) and a '2v1 died to a single Eden' that contradicts the 5v5 macro read at 28
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.rewrite: Your Gideon won mid outright and it carried the early game — the favored lane read held while the rest of the map was even. The watch-out: at 20.4 you were alive and ahead in lane while the team lost 0-3 in the open — shove and rotate there and the numbers flip. When no major is up, don't let the team take that fight short-handed.

3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.quote: Phase died 5 times, well over your usual 2, mostly in fights that were already going badly — the lost Fangtooth at 15.7 and the 28-min throw. Your mitigation was huge (899/min) so the frontline was there; the fix is fight selection, not your build. Don't follow the team into a 4v5 in the open at 20.4 with no objective to justify it.
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.issue: calls 20.4 a 4v5 — the macro read says it was 5v5
3d218c28-1c95-4522-9b96-e5a396c2c2b7.rewrite: Phase died 5 times, well over your usual 2, mostly in fights that were already going badly — your 15.7 death fed their Fangtooth take. Your mitigation was huge (899/min) so the frontline was there; the fix is fight selection, not your build. Don't follow the team into the open-map fights at 20.4 and 28 with no objective to justify them.

503d4ace-557e-44b8-962a-8e3b8d61e889.quote: GRIM.exe carry, 11/4/8 and yours all game vs Sparrow — your best game, but read the macro flags: at 35m and 39m you were alive and ahead in lane and NOT at the fight the team lost a body down. When your lane is that far ahead, shove and rotate — your kills mean nothing farming a side lane while they take Prime and the team fights 4v5.
503d4ace-557e-44b8-962a-8e3b8d61e889.issue: 35.2 was opened 5v4 in your favor per the macro (and you took Orb Prime in that window) — only 39.2 was a body down
503d4ace-557e-44b8-962a-8e3b8d61e889.rewrite: GRIM.exe carry, 11/4/8 and yours all game vs Sparrow — your best game, but read the macro flags: at 35m and 39m you were alive and ahead in lane and NOT at the fight — the 35.2 fight went 1-4 even with a 5v4, and the 39.2 one opened 4v5. When your lane is that far ahead, shove and rotate — your kills mean nothing farming a side lane while the team drops those fights.

59a7f86a-780c-4a2d-9c44-f6b7f0906235.quote: Offlane, support and carry were all read as theirs, but you won on objective tempo and two decisive aces. The 14.4 Fangtooth fight (4-1) flipped the early game, and the 26.8 Primal Fangtooth ace (4-0) closed it — towers ended 10 to 5. Sparrow went 12/4 as the carry. Your one weakness was a pair of open-map bad trades: the 12.9 river loss (0-3) and the 23.7 trade (1-3) with no major up. Both were avoidable — when there's no objective on the line, don't flip a fight you don't have to. The good news is you answered the threat: Aurora (24k magical) was their carry and three of you built magic resist, which is exactly why the late fights went your way.
59a7f86a-780c-4a2d-9c44-f6b7f0906235.issue: Aurora was their offlane, not their carry (their carry was Skylar)
59a7f86a-780c-4a2d-9c44-f6b7f0906235.rewrite: Offlane, support and carry were all read as theirs, but you won on objective tempo and two decisive aces. The 14.4 Fangtooth fight (4-1) flipped the early game, and the 26.8 Primal Fangtooth ace (4-0) closed it — towers ended 10 to 5. Sparrow went 12/4 as the carry. Your one weakness was a pair of open-map bad trades: the 12.9 river loss (0-3) and the 23.7 trade (1-3) with no major up. Both were avoidable — when there's no objective on the line, don't flip a fight you don't have to. The good news is you answered the threat: Aurora (24k magical) was their biggest damage source and three of you built magic resist, which is exactly why the late fights went your way.

61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.quote: Lost the tower trade 7–10 and won anyway — a 28/1 Drongo won 8 fights and cashed 6 into every major on the map.
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.issue: 'every major on the map' overstates — they took 21 majors to your 25
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.rewrite: Lost the tower trade 7–10 and won anyway — a 28/1 Drongo won 8 fights, and 6 of those wins cashed into a prize within 90 seconds, majors finishing 25–21.

61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.quote: This win came from the fights, not the structures: you went 8 won to 1 lost across the game's fights and cashed 6 of the 8 within 90 seconds — Fangtooth at 11, 19 and 24, Primal Fangtooth at 30 and Orb Prime at 33, with a 25–21 kill lead and 79,252 objective damage to their 47,009. Now the honest part of the tower story: they out-towered you 10–7, but not off their fighting — they won exactly one fight all game. The event stream shows 9 of their 10 tower and inhibitor takes landed within 90 seconds of one of your deaths, and 7 of your deaths came outside any fight, caught rotating alone. Repeat the pattern that won it: fight on top of the prize and cash the win. The one sharpen: stop trading solo deaths for their structures — the two uncashed fight wins at 20.6 and 33.1 and the 7 caught-out deaths are the whole gap between a 36-minute grind and a clean close.
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.issue: invents the cashed-prize timeline (Fangtooth at 11/19/24, Primal at 30, Orb at 33 — the 18.6 window actually went to THEIR Mini Prime per the macro), invents which two wins were uncashed (20.6, 33.1), and mislabels the 25–21 majors count as a kill lead
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.rewrite: This win came from the fights, not the structures: you went 8 won to 1 lost across the game's fights and cashed 6 of the 8 into a prize within 90 seconds, finishing 25–21 on majors. Now the honest part of the tower story: they out-towered you 10–7, but not off their fighting — they won exactly one fight all game. Their structure takes rode on your deaths — the death-cost log ties their outer towers, inner towers and inhibitors to deaths at 14.6, 17.1, 20.2, 27.1, 27.8 and 30 — and 7 of your deaths came outside any fight, caught rotating alone. Repeat the pattern that won it: fight on top of the prize and cash the win. The one sharpen: stop trading solo deaths for their structures — the two uncashed fight wins and the 7 caught-out deaths are the whole gap between a 36-minute grind and a clean close.

61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.quote: Fight wins were converted and deaths were not free: your 6 cashed wins bought three Fangtooths, Primal Fangtooth and Orb Prime, while their 10 towers rode almost entirely on your solo deaths (9 of 10 came within 90 seconds of one) — cut the caught-out deaths and the tower count flips with everything else.
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.issue: invents which prizes the 6 cashed wins bought — the SOURCE never names them
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.rewrite: Fight wins were converted and deaths were not free: 6 of your 8 won fights cashed into a prize within 90 seconds, while their tower lead rode on your deaths — the death-cost log ties their outer towers, inner towers and inhibitors to your deaths, 7 of them caught-out, outside any fight. Cut the caught-out deaths and the tower count flips with everything else.

61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.quote: Drongo carry, 28/1/2 — you were the win condition. You took fights alone and won them: 2-0 at their outer tower at 18.6, 3-0 at their inhibitor at 20.6 and again at 28.6 (that one modeled at 3 items online to their side's 11), 2-0 at your Orb Prime at 33.1, on top of 37,403 objective damage and 11.3 CS/min against your 6.5 norm. Your only death, 12.6 to Khaimera, was outside any fight — the exact habit that fed their towers when others did it. Keep everything else.
61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.issue: killer attribution ('to Khaimera') not in SOURCE, and 'took fights alone' overstates the 4v5 macro reads

Your only death, at 12.6, was outside any fight — the exact habit that fed their towers when others did it. Keep everything else.

61dfa250-0851-4d59-8fac-f6b8ea65b044.quote: Aurora jungle, 2/2/10 — you were the frontline: 54,576 damage taken, 46,256 mitigated, 7 wards to your 4 norm, in a jungle matchup that read yours all game against Khaimera. The sharpen is that both deaths were the expensive kind: the 17.1 death preceded their outer tower at 18.5, and the 20.2 catch by Khaimera preceded an inner tower, an inhibitor and a second inner tower inside 90 seconds. And at the 30.6 teamfight you were alive and ahead in lane — shove and rotate there turns the 4v5 the team opened into even numbers.

76a073d7-c105-43d2-9945-aadf1d6c0db5.quote: Mid and jungle were favored and you leaned all the way into objective tempo — 24 objective kills to 17, towers 11 to 6. The won Fangtooth fights stacked up: 11.2 (3-1), 17.3 (3-0), and the close-out at your Primal Fangtooth at 28.4 (4-1) that ended it. Skylar went 13/2 and was the engine. The two losses to learn from were the bad trades: a solo Wukong dying to Gadget at 7.9 (0-2), and the 21.3 river trade (0-2) with no major up. You also dropped the 22.6 Primal Fangtooth contest (3-4) — close, but you bounced back by re-grouping for the 28.4 ace instead of forcing it again. That's the model: when a contest goes bad, reset and take the next one with numbers.
76a073d7-c105-43d2-9945-aadf1d6c0db5.issue: 7.9 was a 5v5 that went 0-2 per the SOURCE — 'a solo Wukong dying to Gadget' invents the victim and killer
76a073d7-c105-43d2-9945-aadf1d6c0db5.rewrite: Mid and jungle were favored and you leaned all the way into objective tempo — 24 objective kills to 17, towers 11 to 6. The won Fangtooth fights stacked up: 11.2 (3-1), 17.3 (3-0), and the close-out at your Primal Fangtooth at 28.4 (4-1) that ended it. Skylar went 13/2 and was the engine. The two losses to learn from were the bad trades: the 7.9 open-map loss (0-2) started while your support was pinned in a losing lane, and the 21.3 river trade (0-2) with no major up. You also dropped the 22.6 Primal Fangtooth contest (3-4) — close, but you bounced back by re-grouping for the 28.4 ace instead of forcing it again. That's the model: when a contest goes bad, reset and take the next one with numbers.

c14651e7-3549-45a7-aa2d-544089ff317e.quote: You actually controlled the late objective board — you took Fangtooth at 6, 13 and 18 and Primal Fangtooth at 24 and 29 — but it didn't matter because you lost the team fights around them. The decisive one was the 13.6 Fangtooth fight (2-4) that gave Shinbi and The Fey the early lead, and the game ended on a 32.2 fight at their Orb Prime (1-3). In between, a bad trade at their half (22.4, 1-4) and a 24.2 loss on your own Primal Fangtooth (0-2) kept the bleeding going. The Fey (34k magical) was their carry and only one of you built magic resist. Towers: 3 to their 9. Taking objectives is good — but you have to win the fight first, and you kept contesting majors without the bodies to hold them.
c14651e7-3549-45a7-aa2d-544089ff317e.issue: The Fey was their midlane, not their carry (their carry was Sparrow)

ca184f0b-f403-40b0-a23c-532eefce7ce9.quote: This one was winnable: the enemy ran only 1 of 5 on their meta core, so the draft edge was actually yours (carry, mid and support all favored). You even won the opening fight at 5.7 (3-2). It slipped on fight selection and one build hole — Countess (32k magical) was their carry and only one of you itemized magic resist, so she kept blowing you up. The decisive loss was the 22.4 fight at their Fangtooth (0-3), which let them roll Genesis Core, Orb Prime and Primal Fangtooth in a row, and the game ended on a 29-min bad trade deep in your own jungle (2-4). Towers: 4 to their 9. With an off-meta enemy, the play was to stop force-fighting Countess and build the one resist item that turns her off.
ca184f0b-f403-40b0-a23c-532eefce7ce9.issue: 'Genesis Core' is not an objective in the SOURCE (death costs show only Orb Prime and Primal Fangtooth after 22.4), and Countess was their midlane, not their carry

Towers: 4 to their 9. With an off-meta enemy, the play was to stop force-fighting Countess and build the one resist item that turns her off.
